reasoning: The market threshold is the previous high water mark from the cutoff date, 116.1. The fetch tool indicates the current stock price on 2025-10-24 is 116.07. The upcoming timeline contains Q3 earnings set for roughly Oct 30, before the Nov 2 resolution. Analysts have recently downgraded KMB due to revenue guidance cuts (16% drop expected according to some sources, 3.6% annual in others), tariff impacts, and cost concerns, which has pushed the stock to a 1-year low near mid-110s. The linear regression predicts a $114 price by early Nov. Therefore, the probability of exceeding the exact hurdle of 116.1 on Nov 2 is modestly below 0.5. I estimate ~42%. By late Nov (Nov 25), some post-earnings reversion to the mean from an oversold condition might push it back up slightly, raising the probability towards ~0.45. By late Jan (Jan 24, 2026), long-term drift comes more into play, though an early December ex-dividend roughly drops the price by $1.26, mechanically offsetting the upward drift. This keeps the Jan 24 estimate slightly below 50/50, around 0.48, reflecting increased uncertainty over the longer horizon.
